title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer raises three issues: (1) the regex validation in `verify_release_config.sh` is too strict and will reject valid stestr patterns starting with `^`, `.`, or digits — it should only enforce non-empty/non-comment lines; (2) the test-excludes validation is hardcoded to a single file path instead of iterating over all `<release>/test-excludes/*.txt` files; and (3) the `Resolve source ref` step in `build-images.yaml` uses `yq` which isn't available on a fresh `ubuntu-latest` runner and needs an explicit install step.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The `test_test_excludes_file_format` check in `verify_release_config.sh` is overly restrictive (`grep -vE '^[0-9]+:(#|[a-zA-Z_])'`), which will flag perfectly valid stestr regex patterns that start with `^`, `.`, digits, or other metacharacters as invalid; consider loosening this to only enforce "comment or non-empty" semantics without constraining the first character of regex lines.
- The test-excludes validation in `verify_release_config.sh` is hard-wired to `releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt`, which means additional services or future releases won't be validated in the same way; you may want to parameterize this to iterate over all `<release>/test-excludes/*.txt` files instead of a single path.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The `test_test_excludes_file_format` check in `verify_release_config.sh` is overly restrictive (`grep -vE '^[0-9]+:(#|[a-zA-Z_])'`), which will flag perfectly valid stestr regex patterns that start with `^`, `.`, digits, or other metacharacters as invalid; consider loosening this to only enforce "comment or non-empty" semantics without constraining the first character of regex lines.
- The test-excludes validation in `verify_release_config.sh` is hard-wired to `releases/2025.2/test-excludes/keystone.txt`, which means additional services or future releases won't be validated in the same way; you may want to parameterize this to iterate over all `<release>/test-excludes/*.txt` files instead of a single path.

## Individual Comments

### Comment 1
<location path=".github/workflows/build-images.yaml" line_range="517-522" />
<code_context>
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
+
+      - name: Resolve source ref
+        id: source-ref
+        env:
+          MATRIX_SERVICE: ${{ matrix.service }}
+          MATRIX_RELEASE: ${{ matrix.release }}
+        run: |
+          ref=$(yq ".\"${MATRIX_SERVICE}\"" "releases/${MATRIX_RELEASE}/source-refs.yaml")
+          if [ -z "$ref" ] || [ "$ref" = "null" ]; then
</code_context>
<issue_to_address>
**issue (bug_risk):** The `Resolve source ref` step assumes `yq` is available, but this job doesn't install it.

On a fresh `ubuntu-latest` runner, `yq` is not installed by default, so this step will fail when it runs:

```bash
ref=$(yq ".\"${MATRIX_SERVICE}\"" "releases/${MATRIX_RELEASE}/source-refs.yaml")
```
Please add an explicit `yq` installation/setup step (e.g. `mikefarah/yq` or package install) before this, or switch to a tool that’s guaranteed to be available on the runner.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

***

code_comments.body:
**issue (bug_risk):** The `Resolve source ref` step assumes `yq` is available, but this job doesn't install it.

On a fresh `ubuntu-latest` runner, `yq` is not installed by default, so this step will fail when it runs:

```bash
ref=$(yq ".\"${MATRIX_SERVICE}\"" "releases/${MATRIX_RELEASE}/source-refs.yaml")
```
Please add an explicit `yq` installation/setup step (e.g. `mikefarah/yq` or package install) before this, or switch to a tool that’s guaranteed to be available on the runner.
code_comments.author: sourcery-ai[bot]
